page: Amazon Web Services r8id.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 64 virtual CPUs derived from 32 physical cores with 2 threads per core, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz and backed by a 480 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. For local storage, it includes 3800 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and it operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple domains. The server achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 2178.0 and a multi-core score of 20973.0, both placing in the top 10% tier. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 5121.18 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 191.69 GB/sec for large-block reads. Database operations are robust, with Passmark database operations scoring 28597.34 and Redis SET operations reaching 112.39 million operations per second. Additionally, the CPU's Advanced Matrix Extensions support high-speed LLM inference, processing prompts at 296.05 tokens per second on a medium 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8id.16xlarge delivers high resource density by combining massive DDR5 memory capacity with fast local NVMe SSD storage on a single instance. This configuration minimizes the need for external network-attached storage for high-throughput workloads, optimizing the performance-per-resource ratio. The hardware profile and benchmark results make this instance highly suitable for memory-intensive databases, large-scale Redis caching layers, high-throughput static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r8id.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is configured with 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz and includes 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps. Benchmark results show top-tier performance, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 20973.0 and static web serving throughput of 108.46 GB/sec. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX, enabling efficient prompt processing for large language models. With its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and fast local storage, this instance is highly suited for memory-intensive databases, high-throughput web serving, and large-scale caching applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8id.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for high-throughput database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8id.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD local storage.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized platform featuring fast local NVMe storage and advanced vector extensions for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 3.9 GHz processor with 480 MB L3 cache
512 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
3800 GB of direct-attached NVMe SSD local instance storage
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier performance in database operations and static web serving
Intel AMX support for accelerated CPU-based AI and inference workloads
